author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter XVII. 6–8.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this discourse we purpose speaking, as He gives us grace, on these words of the Lord which run thus: “I have manifested Thy name unto the men whom Thou gavest me out of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If He said this only of those disciples with whom He had supped, and to whom, before beginning His prayer, He had said so much, it can have nothing to do with that clarification, or, as others have translated it, glorification, whereof He was previously speaking, and whereby the Son clarifies or glorifies the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what great glory, or what like glory, was it to become known to twelve, or rather eleven mortal creatures?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if, in saying, “I have manifested Thy name unto the men whom Thou gavest me out of the world,” He wished all to be understood, even those who were still to believe on Him, as belonging to His great Church which was yet to be made up of all nations, and of which it is said in the psalm, “I will confess to Thee in the great Church [congregation];” it is plainly that glorification wherewith the Son glorifies the Father, when He makes His name known to all nations and to so many generations of men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what He says here, “I have manifested Thy name unto the men whom Thou gavest me out of the world,” is similar to what He had said a little before, “I have glorified Thee upon the earth”, (ver.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: 4); putting both here and there the past for the future, as One who knew that it was predestinated to be done, and therefore saying that He had done what He had still to do, though without any uncertainty, in the future.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what follows makes it more credible that His words, “I have manifested Thy name to the men whom Thou gavest me out of the world,” were spoken by Him of those who were already His disciples, and not of all who were yet to believe on Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For after these words, He added: “Thine they were, and Thou gavest them me; and they have kept Thy word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now they have known that all things, whatsoever Thou hast given me, are of Thee: for I have given unto them the words which Thou gavest me; and they have received them, and have known surely that I came out from Thee, and they have believed that Thou didst send me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Although all these words also might have been said of all believers still to come, when that which was now a matter of hope had been turned into fact, inasmuch as they were words that still pointed to the future; yet we are impelled the more to understand Him as uttering them only of those who were at that time His disciples, by what He says shortly afterwards: “While I was with them, I kept them in Thy name: those that Thou gavest me I have kept, and none of them is lost, but the son of perdition; that the Scripture might be fulfilled” (ver.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: 12); meaning Judas, who betrayed Him, for He was the only one of the apostolic twelve that perished.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And then He adds, “And now come I to Thee,” from which it is manifest that it was of His own bodily presence that He said, “While I was with them, I kept them,” as if already that presence were no longer with them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in this way He wished to intimate His own ascension as in the immediate future, when He said, “And now come I to Thee:” going, that is, to the Father’s right hand; whence He is hereafter to come to judge the quick and the dead in the self-same bodily presence, according to the rule of faith and sound doctrine: for in His spiritual presence He was still, of course, to be with them after His ascension, and with the whole of His Church in this world even to the end of time.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We cannot, therefore, rightly understand of whom He said, “While I was with them, I kept them,” save as those only who believed on Him, whom He had already begun to keep by His bodily presence, but was now to leave without it, in order that He might keep them with the Father by His spiritual presence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thereafter, indeed, He also unites with them the rest of His disciples, when He says, “Neither pray I for these alone, but for those also who shall believe on me through their word.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where He shows still more clearly that He was not speaking before of all who belonged to Him, in the passage where He saith, “I have manifested Thy name unto the men whom Thou gavest me,” but of those only who were listening to Him when He so spake.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From the very outset, therefore, of His prayer, when “He lifted up His eyes to heaven, and said, Father, the hour is come; glorify Thy Son, that Thy Son also may glorify Thee,” on to what He said a little afterwards, “And now, O Father, glorify Thou me with Thine own self with the glory which I had with Thee before the world was,” He wished all His disciples to be understood, to whom He makes the Father known, and thereby glorifies Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For after saying, “That Thy Son may glorify Thee,” He straightway showed how that was to be done, by adding, “As Thou hast given Him power over all flesh, that He should give eternal life to as many as Thou hast given Him: and this is life eternal, that they might know Thee the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom Thou hast sent.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Father cannot be glorified through any knowledge attained by men, unless He also be known by whom He is glorified, that is to say, by whom He is made known to the nations of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The glorification of the Father is not that which was displayed in connection with the apostles only, but that which is displayed in all men, of whom as His members Christ is the head.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the words cannot be understood as applied to the apostles only, “As Thou hast given Him power over all flesh, that He should give eternal life to as many as Thou hast given Him;” but to all, assuredly, on whom, as believing on Him, eternal life is bestowed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, let us now see what He says about those disciples of His who were then listening to Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I have manifested,” He says, “Thy name unto the men whom Thou gavest me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did they not, then, know the name of God when they were Jews?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what of that which we read, “God is known in Judah; His name is great in Israel”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, “I have manifested Thy name unto these men whom Thou gavest me out of the world,” and who are now hearing my words: not that name of Thine whereby Thou art called God, but that whereby Thou art called my Father: a name that could not be manifested without the manifestation of the Son Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this name of God, by which He is called, could not but be known in some way to the whole creation, and so to every nation, before they believed in Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For such is the energy of true Godhead, that it cannot be altogether and utterly hidden from any rational creature, so long as it makes use of its reason.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, with the exception of a few in whom nature has become outrageously depraved, the whole race of man acknowledges God as the maker of this world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In respect, therefore, of His being the maker of this world that is visible in heaven and earth around us, God was known unto all nations even before they were indoctrinated into the faith of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in this respect, that He was not, without grievous wrong being done to Himself, to be worshipped alongside of false gods, God was known in Judah alone.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in respect of His being the Father of this Christ, by whom He taketh away the sin of the world, this name of His, previously kept secret from all, He now made manifest to those whom the Father Himself had given Him out of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But how had He done so, if the hour were not yet come, of which He had formerly said that the hour would come, “when I shall no more speak unto you proverbs, but I shall show you plainly of my Father”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Can it be supposed that the proverbs themselves contained such a plain anouncement?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, then, is it said, “I will declare to you openly,” but just because that “in proverbs” is not “openly”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when it is no longer concealed in proverbs, but uttered in plain words, then without a doubt it is spoken openly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, then, had He manifested what He had not as yet openly declared?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It must be understood, therefore, in this way, that the past tense is put for the future, like those other words, “All things that I have heard of my Father, I have made known unto you:” as something He had not yet done, but spoke of as if He had, because His doing of it He knew to be infallibly pre-determined.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what are we to make of the words, “Whom Thou gavest me out of the world”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is said of them that they were not of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this they attained to by regeneration, and not by generation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what, also, of that which follows, “Thine they were, and Thou gavest them me”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was there a time when they belonged to the Father, and not to His only-begotten Son; and had the Father once on a time anything apart from the Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless, there was a time when God the Son had something, which that same Son as man possessed not; for He had not yet become man of an earthly mother, when He possessed all things in common with the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore in saying, “Thine they were,” there is thereby no self-disruption made by God the Son, apart from whom there was nothing ever possessed by the Father; but it is His custom to attribute all the power He possesses to Him, of whom He Himself is, who has the power.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For of whom He has it that He is, of Him He has it that He is able; and both together He always had, for He never had being without having ability.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, what ever the Father could [do], always side by side with Him could the Son; since He, who never had being without having ability, was never without the Father, as the Father never was without Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And thus, as the Father is eternally omnipotent, so is the Son co-eternally omnipotent; and if all-powerful, certainly all-possessing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For such rather, if we would speak exactly, is the word by which we translate what is called by the Greeks παντοκράτωρ which our writers would not interpret by the term omnipotent, seeing that παντοκράτωρ is all-possessing, were it not that they felt it to be equivalent in meaning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, could the eternal all-possessing ever have, that the co-eternal all-possessing had not likewise?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In saying, therefore, “And Thou gavest them me,” He intimated that it was as man He had received this power to have them; seeing that He, who was always omnipotent, was not always man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, while He seems rather to have attributed it to the Father, that He received them from Him, since all that is, is of Him, of whom He is; yet He also gave them to Himself, that is, Christ, God with the Father, gave men to the manhood of Christ, which had not its being with the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Finally, He who says in this place, “Thine they were, and Thou gavest them me,” had already said in a previous passage to the same disciples, “I have chosen you out of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here, then, let every carnal thought be crushed and annihilated.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Son says that the men were given Him by the Father out of the world, to whom He says elsewhere, “I have chosen you out of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those whom God the Son chose along with the Father out of the world, the very same Son as man received out of the world from the Father; for the Father had not given them to the Son had He not chosen them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And in this way, as the Son did not thereby set the Father aside, when He said, “I have chosen you out of the world,” seeing that they were simultaneously chosen by the Father also: as little did He thereby exclude Himself, when He said, “Thine they were,” for they were equally also the property of the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now that same Son as man received those who belonged not to Himself, because He also as God received a servant-form which was not originally His own.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He proceeds to say, “And they have kept Thy word: now they have known that all things, whatsoever Thou hast given me, are of Thee;” that is, they have known that I am of Thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Father gave all things at the very time when He begat Him who was to have all things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For I have given unto them,” He says, “the words which Thou gavest me; and they have received them;” that is, they have understood and kept hold of them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the word is received when it is perceived by the mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And they have known truly,” He adds, “that I came out from Thee, and they have believed that Thou didst send me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this last clause we must also supply “truly;” for when He said, “They have known truly,” He intended its explanation by adding, “and they have believed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That, therefore, “they have believed truly” which “they have known truly;” just as “I came out from Thee” is the same as “Thou didst send me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When, therefore, He said, “They have known truly,” lest any might suppose that such a knowledge was already acquired by sight, and not by faith, He subjoined the explanation, “And they have believed,” so that we should supply “truly,” and understand the saying, “They have known truly,” as equivalent to “They have believed truly:” not in the way which He intimated shortly before, when He said, “Do ye now believe?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The hour cometh, and is now come, that ye shall be scattered, every man to his own, and shall leave me alone.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But “they have believed truly,” that is, in the way it ought to be believed, without constraint, with firmness, constancy, and fortitude: no longer now to go to their own, and leave Christ alone.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As yet, indeed, the disciples were not of the character He here describes in words of the past tense, as if they were so already, but as thereby declaring beforehand what sort they were yet to be, namely, when they had received the Holy Spirit, who, according to the promise, should teach them all things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how was it, before they received the Spirit, that they kept that word of His which He spake regarding them, as if they had done so, when the chief of them thrice denied Him, after hearing from His lips the future fate of the man who denied Him before men?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He had given them, therefore, as He said, the words which the Father gave Him; but when at length they received them spiritually, not in an outward way with their ears, but inwardly in their hearts, then they truly received them, for then they truly knew them; and they truly knew them, because they truly believed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what human language will suffice to explain how the Father gave those words to the Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The question, of course, will appear easier if we suppose Him to have received such words in His capacity as the Son of man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet, although thus born of the Virgin, who will undertake to relate when and how it was that He learned them, since even that very generation which He had of the Virgin who will venture to declare?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if our idea be that He received these words of the Father in His capacity as begotten of, and co-eternal with, the Father, let us then exclude all such thoughts of time as if He existed previous to His possessing them, and so received the possession of that which He had not before; for whatever God the Father gave to God the Son, He gave in the act of begetting.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Father gave those things to the Son without which He could not be the Son, in the same manner as He gave Him being itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how otherwise would He give any words to the Word, wherein in an ineffable way He hath spoken all things?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now, in reference to what follows, you must defer your expectations till another discourse.
